117769.explanation: In March 2026 a new municipal council will be elected. This council has the mandate from the residents to govern the municipality for the next 4 years. Of course, involvement and participation of the residents must take place in the various decision-making processes.

117772.explanation: The opening hours (especially the closing times of these establishments) must be done with consideration for the surroundings. WE support lively village centers, but we also grant our residents the necessary night rest. So together we must come to an agreement if something other than the current situation is desired.

117773.explanation: WE Lansingerland stands for a sustainable society. Making homes, the environment, or mobility more sustainable must be doable for everyone. We pursue sustainability with wisdom. We want to stimulate the sustainability of existing homes.

117775.explanation: Based on our legal obligation and moral responsibility, we ensure that newcomers receive and have a safe and suitable home, with targeted guidance and support for successful integration. The current asylum reception (Tobias) is appropriate and works without problems.

117778.explanation: Living well and healthily in Lansingerland is essential. We must look out for each other. Through good information, the use of open fireplaces and wood stoves should be limited to improve air quality.

117780.explanation: From 2026 onwards, there may be a national fireworks ban. It could be a challenge for Lansingerland society to look for a new New Year's Eve tradition.

117781.explanation: In 2026 the new GIS (replacing the current waste collection station) will open. This will offer even more service. Residents know their way well to the ABS (soon GIS). Free home collection of bulky waste will increase costs, which will affect the rates.

117782.explanation: The current rules work well. We are proud of the events in our municipality. But we must continue being considerate of each other so that it remains enjoyable for everyone.

117783.explanation: Entrepreneurs themselves are responsible for this. If the municipality takes over these costs, it means cuts will have to be made elsewhere.

117787.explanation: Music education should be part of the standard curriculum. Subsidizing music lessons should be limited to lower incomes to ensure equal opportunity for every child.

117791.explanation: WIJ is cautious about increasing the use of cameras. No cameras in public spaces unless... WIJ agrees, as is already done, to deploy temporary mobile cameras at special locations and events if necessary.

117793.explanation: WIJ's principle is: our community enforcement officers (BOAs) first talk and only fine or intervene if necessary. Providing a means of force could cause a shift. If the situation becomes too threatening for our BOAs, it becomes a police task.

117795.explanation: WIJ wants our residents (spectators, youth, seniors, etc.) to preferably be prioritized first. The waiting lists for our residents are too long. They want to continue living in their own environment, so priority is justified.

117798.explanation: This sounds very nice but in Lansingerland there are virtually no vacant offices and if there are, owners have such economic interests that they can cover the costs themselves. No public money needs to be used.

117799.explanation: Lansingerland previously built LOO1. This also provides space for urgent seekers. The question is whether we should build these homes for students or for starters and urgent seekers needing small housing. Container homes are a supplement to the housing supply.

117800.explanation: WIJ prefers to focus on having meaningful daily activities (work, volunteer work). Unfortunately, this will not be possible in all cases. But where possible, we must collectively give that resident a push to participate as fully as possible.

117801.explanation: Unfortunately, not everyone is financially well off. It is our shared responsibility to help those who have less or give them the necessary support.

117806.explanation: A blue zone in the centers is necessary, but outside the centers no blue zones or parking permits.

117807.explanation: There is no utility or necessity.

117811.explanation: In Lansingerland, less property tax (ozb) is levied than the national government expects from us; we are below the national average. WIJ therefore advocates managing local taxes wisely and never higher than the national average.

117814.explanation: WIJ stands for good entrepreneurial policy with a healthy and strong business climate. Where possible, expansion of business parks is allowed.

117816.explanation: It is currently a legal obligation.

117819.explanation: We have a beautiful swimming pool in Lansingerland with a large sunbathing area. The pool must certainly remain up-to-date and possibly be further sustainable. Further renewal is not currently needed.

117825.explanation: Fortunately, the three villages in Lansingerland each have their own specific center with a unique character.

117827.explanation: The attractiveness of the Bleiswijk village center includes good parking. We must keep it that way. This increases not only attractiveness but also livability and economic strength.

117828.explanation: Also in Bleiswijk, starters, residents with low or middle incomes, and seniors want to live (or continue living) there. Appropriate and affordable housing is important to be able to provide a place for everyone.

117829.explanation: The municipality invests well in current youth work. WIJ continues to find prevention through good support to prevent loneliness, debts, and other problems important.

117769.explanation: Leefbaar 3B finds resident participation on impactful themes essential. If there is much debate on a topic, residents should be able to express themselves in an advisory referendum.

117772.explanation: Entrepreneurs should be able to conduct business freely; they know best what fits their company and surroundings. However, this must be within the limits set by the municipality in the APV.

117773.explanation: Leefbaar 3B wants realistic sustainability. More solar panels on roofs of homes, businesses, and schools. Where possible, we support collective purchasing or solar panels on municipal buildings. However, we should not go overboard with high costs for the municipality.

117775.explanation: Leefbaar 3B opposes large-scale asylum reception in Lansingerland. If the Dispersion Act is definitively repealed, the current reception behind the town hall (Tobias 2) should not be extended beyond 2030.

117778.explanation: Use should be allowed, with attention to health and nuisance. No generic ban.

117780.explanation: There will be a national ban on fireworks. For this reason, Leefbaar 3B supports a central fireworks show per village center. Illegal fireworks should be enforced against as much as possible.

117781.explanation: For Leefbaar 3B, it is negotiable to have, for example, one free bulky waste collection per year. However, waste policy must remain affordable. Residents should be able to dispose of waste easily without hassle. But illegal dumping must always be tackled firmly.

117782.explanation: Events belong to our lively municipality. Leefbaar 3B envisions a future where our village centers again buzz with events and weekly markets, where young and old come together. Of course, everything must be within legal limits and considerate of residents.

117783.explanation: We make public buildings and sports facilities accessible to people with physical disabilities. We help entrepreneurs remove barriers as much as possible from the municipality, but they are themselves responsible for their business and making their buildings accessible.

117787.explanation: This is already possible through the Youth Fund for Sports & Culture. Leefbaar 3B wants to maintain this fund. This keeps participation not only in music lessons but also dance and theater accessible for every child whose parents cannot afford it.

117791.explanation: Camera surveillance is a necessary tool to combat crime and nuisance. Leefbaar 3B wants to expand the network of security cameras at strategic locations.

117793.explanation: Leefbaar 3B primarily focuses on prevention and protection of BOAs through training, de-escalation, self-defense, and bodycams. The monopoly on force lies with the police. Only with concrete cause can a temporary pilot with extra means be considered in exceptional cases.

117795.explanation: Our residents, including our youth, must be prioritized when renting social or mid-segment rental housing or buying a new home.

117798.explanation: There is hardly any vacancy in Lansingerland. Selling homes is lucrative enough; subsidies are unnecessarily burdensome for the municipality. If someone wants to convert their building into housing, the municipality should be willing and available to cooperate as much as possible for a smooth process.

117799.explanation: Young people should be prioritized for rental homes and studios: preferably not in containers, but in good and full-fledged housing. These can also be prefab homes. For our residents who urgently need shelter, full-fledged homes for temporary use must be available.

117800.explanation: People who use social assistance and can participate well should provide a return service: for example, at clubs, supervised bicycle parking, or in the municipality’s outdoor services.

117801.explanation: A society is only as strong as its weakest links, and therefore Leefbaar 3B lets no one fall. Our social policy is humane and results-oriented: we look after the elderly, people with disabilities, vulnerable families, and everyone who needs support.

117806.explanation: We give the car the space it needs without losing sight of cyclists and pedestrians. Where a permit is necessary for residents to keep a neighborhood livable, this permit must be free or as cheap as possible and valid for multiple years.

117807.explanation: The car is an important means of transport for many residents: for commuting, shopping, visiting family, or sports matches. Leefbaar 3B does not want to penalize car ownership. Therefore, there remains space for cars in Lansingerland: good access roads, enough parking, and clear signposting.

117811.explanation: Leefbaar 3B feels local taxes for our residents and entrepreneurs should remain reasonable by keeping property tax (ozb) as low as possible.

117814.explanation: Lansingerland has limited space left and already has many business parks with many entrepreneurs. Expansion does not seem necessary. Leefbaar 3B wants existing sites maintained well, with sufficient parking and continued focus on safety.

117816.explanation: No automatic priority for status holders! Urgency and priority must be tailored, not automatically given based on a label. Everyone should be treated under the same conditions when allocating housing and be assigned housing that meets their current needs.

117819.explanation: The swimming pool must be kept well-maintained and become energy neutral as soon as possible. The municipality must anticipate well on growth and future use of the pool.

117825.explanation: Leefbaar 3B believes there should be a community center in every village. After years of policy discarding social property, there must again be space for social initiatives and associations. Village centers strengthen cohesion and livability.

117827.explanation: The Dorpsstraat in Bleiswijk must become low-traffic: with input from entrepreneurs and residents, Bleiswijk’s center should become a vibrant hub. No more car traffic except for residents. Kranenburgplein should become an attraction through hospitality and events like a weekly market.

117828.explanation: There is an urgent need for mid-range and affordable owner-occupied homes in Lansingerland. Therefore, each housing development must consider a healthy mix in terms of location and financial feasibility.

117829.explanation: Professional youth workers are indispensable for early detection and support. Leefbaar 3B wants more entertainment for young people within the municipality and believes there should be a youth meeting place in every village.

117769.explanation: CDA Lansingerland supports representative democracy and is therefore cautious regarding the use of referenda.

117772.explanation: Entrepreneurs deserve space and low regulatory pressure. At the same time, residents deserve a good night's rest, requiring appropriate closing times.

117773.explanation: CDA wants a municipality-wide heating network that growers and residents who want it can connect to. The municipality can co-finance its construction from the proceeds of selling our shares in Eneco.

117775.explanation: CDA Lansingerland takes responsibility for asylum reception, but in a careful manner: not in the middle of residential areas, but in a logical location at the municipality’s edge, with Bleizo being the most suitable location as long as it is undeveloped.

117778.explanation: The municipality should provide information about the impact of stoves on air quality, as residents can be affected by this. But a ban goes too far for us.

117780.explanation: Thanks to the national CDA, there remains room for organizations and neighborhood associations to organize fireworks shows together. We want our municipality to cooperate as well. It is not necessary for the government to organize this itself.

117781.explanation: Taking bulky waste to the dump is free and must remain free. For bulky waste collection, the municipality incurs costs and a (small) fee may be charged.

117782.explanation: The existing rules to combat noise nuisance function sufficiently.

117783.explanation: The government must improve accessibility of buildings, streets, and buses so everyone can move around effortlessly. Sidewalks must also be wide enough. A subsidy for entrepreneurs is not needed. They can invest in this themselves.

117787.explanation: CDA Lansingerland supports cultural and music lessons in schools and favors a modest subsidy for choirs, orchestras, and music venues. But not every child needs a subsidy.

117791.explanation: Using cameras is an important tool for CDA Lansingerland in the fight against crime and nuisance. Think of bicycle parking at metro stations and reducing nuisance along the Rotte.

117793.explanation: Borough officers (BOAs) are important for maintaining public order. We want to increase their visibility and better support them with additional training and resources, so they can act effectively against nuisance, vandalism, and minor crime.

117795.explanation: We want to build for the community: homes are for Lansingerlanders, not for profit or speculation. Local first-time buyers always receive structural priority in new projects.

117798.explanation: Housing construction preserving the village character is our top priority. If offices can be converted and require a subsidy, we are open to that.

117799.explanation: We support building temporary housing (flex homes) for young people. We also make it easier to split existing homes or rent rooms (lodging rental).

117800.explanation: We expect a counter-performance from residents receiving social assistance benefits. That can start by volunteering at the sports club bar or serving coffee in the nursing home.

117801.explanation: Residents with incomes at social assistance level or only state pension (AOW) without additional pension have and keep the right to exemption from local taxes, in our opinion.

117806.explanation: Sufficient parking space in new neighborhoods is important. Also, for well-functioning centers in the three villages, enough free parking spaces are vital.

117807.explanation: This is more suitable for busy, large cities like Rotterdam.

117811.explanation: For CDA Lansingerland, it is paramount that property tax (ozb) is applied fairly, transparently, and moderately. Ozb rates remain moderate and predictable without sudden increases.

117814.explanation: There must be enough space for entrepreneurs in every village. But CDA Lansingerland does not want (heavy) industry near residential areas. That is why we have opposed, together with residents, the council’s plans to make space for heavy industry near Warmoeziersweg, Chrysantenweg, and Molenweg.

117816.explanation: Housing for status holders must be spread out and appropriate to the scale of a neighborhood. Not too many on one street or in one neighborhood. No large-scale concentrations like buildings with 40 or more status holders at one location.

117819.explanation: Fully making De Windas swimming pool sustainable is high on our wishlist: the pool is the largest gas consumer among municipal buildings.

117825.explanation: We highly value vital village centers with space for entrepreneurship, meeting, and relaxation. The unique character of the three villages is central. The government must ensure attractive outdoor spaces and good accessibility by foot, bike, and car.

117827.explanation: We highly value vital village centers with space for entrepreneurship, meeting, and relaxation. The government must ensure attractive outdoor spaces and good accessibility by foot, bike, and car. A car-free center does not fit in Bleiswijk.

117828.explanation: CDA was the first party to advocate large-scale new construction in Bleiswijk, at Lange Vaart and Merenweg. Affordability is paramount: at least half to two-thirds of new construction in sustainable social rent, mid-rent, and affordable owner-occupied homes below the NHG limit.

117829.explanation: To offer enough entertainment for older youth, Youth Work Lansingerland organizes a wide range of activities in The Point in Bergschenhoek and The Hangout in Bleiswijk. We want to keep it that way but without extra budget.

117769.explanation: VVD Lansingerland values citizen participation highly, but opposes referenda because complex issues are reduced to a simple yes-or-no choice, causing nuance to be lost.

117772.explanation: Entrepreneurs must have the freedom to determine their own opening hours, with consideration for the environment and in good consultation with neighbors.

117773.explanation: The VVD encourages local and collective energy initiatives that promote innovation and relieve the energy grid. The municipality should provide space and support where effective, but not automatically financial aid.

117775.explanation: VVD Lansingerland opposes the establishment of an asylum seekers center in the municipality and therefore wants the existing center to close as soon as possible.

117778.explanation: The government should be cautious about banning private choices. At the same time, a fireplace or wood stove can cause nuisance to neighbors if used improperly. VVD Lansingerland finds it normal to always consider the surroundings when using a fireplace or wood stove.

117780.explanation: If a fireworks ban is introduced, it is good that associations or entrepreneurs take initiatives themselves. Organizing a fireworks show is not a municipal task.

117781.explanation: In Lansingerland, you can bring bulky waste for free. It is logical to charge fees for bulky waste collection.

117782.explanation: Festivals and parties are already subject to strict permit policies, with sound standards carefully monitored.

117783.explanation: Entrepreneurs are themselves responsible for the accessibility of their buildings.

117787.explanation: VVD Lansingerland believes that sports and culture should be accessible to everyone. Residents can use support such as Stichting Leergeld or the Youth Fund for Sports and Culture with help from the municipality.

117791.explanation: Camera surveillance at high-risk locations, such as shopping areas, public transport stops, and unsafe intersections, helps prevent and resolve incidents.

117793.explanation: Borough officers de-escalate where possible but must be well trained and equipped with body cameras and batons.

117795.explanation: VVD Lansingerland wants to use the maximum legal scope to prioritize local residents for social rental housing so that Lansingerland residents can continue living in Lansingerland.

117798.explanation: The municipality can support such initiatives with permits and active cooperation. However, it is not the municipality's task to convert office buildings into homes.

117799.explanation: Student housing is not a task for the municipality.

117800.explanation: The VVD believes everyone should contribute according to their ability. People receiving welfare benefits provide a return by doing socially meaningful work. Those who cannot work receive appropriate support. Those who can work but refuse may have their benefits reduced.

117801.explanation: Income policy is not a task of the municipality, and waiving taxes is not a structural solution. Poverty can cause problems in many areas of life; therefore, the municipality must offer low-threshold support or refer to volunteer organizations.

117806.explanation: VVD Lansingerland is against any form of paid parking within the municipality.

117807.explanation: Such large-city problems do not occur in Lansingerland.

117811.explanation: The VVD's position is that the property tax (ozb) must not increase.

117814.explanation: In some locations within the municipality, business parks can still be expanded. VVD Lansingerland opposes expanding business parks if it comes at the expense of housing and green spaces.

117816.explanation: No priority for status holders on housing. Residents of Lansingerland who have been on the waiting list for a long time should get a home first.

117819.explanation: Swimming pool De Windas must be well maintained, safe, and remain attractive. Renewal is not necessary; the pool is only 17 years old.

117825.explanation: All communities in Lansingerland already have a shopping center, a community living room, and a cultural center. These are important meeting places for residents. VVD Lansingerland wants to preserve these.

117827.explanation: Cars must remain welcome in the center of Bleiswijk.

117828.explanation: There is a growing need for affordable housing throughout Lansingerland. More small apartments, life-course-proof homes, 'kangaroo homes,' and maisonettes must be built so there is suitable housing for every budget.

117829.explanation: VVD Lansingerland believes youth work should be maintained as it currently is. Youth work plays a key role in preventing youth problems through low-threshold guidance and support. It also has an important signaling function.

117769.explanation: We value residents' opinions through participation and focus on this so that a referendum becomes unnecessary. From the program: "There is genuine citizen participation, which you notice in the attitude of the staff. Residents are involved early in the decision-making process."

117772.explanation: This is not an urgent issue in Lansingerland. We look at each situation to determine what fits best with livability and the environment.

117773.explanation: We encourage local electricity generation and use; if people generate their own power in a cooperative, store it in a neighborhood battery, and use it themselves, the grid is also not burdened.

117775.explanation: Lansingerland also shelters people who have fled war, violence, and persecution. How we do this says something about who we are as a community. Good reception is not a favor but a shared responsibility.

117778.explanation: Wood burning causes nuisance and health problems. We focus on discouragement and awareness, not a generic ban.

117780.explanation: Safety and social cohesion are important, but this requires a broader consideration than a single standard solution.

117781.explanation: We want to collect bulky waste in a low-threshold manner to prevent dumping but choose a tailored approach. Not unlimited free collection, but smartly organized and affordable for the municipality.

117782.explanation: Events must remain fun without causing nuisance. Protection of health and the living environment comes first.

117783.explanation: Accessibility is the responsibility of entrepreneurs, but the municipality helps where necessary to remove barriers. This way, we ensure everyone can participate.

117787.explanation: Culture is for everyone: our municipality participates in the Youth Culture Fund, which enables children from less affluent families to take dance, theater, or music lessons, for example.

117791.explanation: Camera surveillance, preventive searching, area bans, and assembly prohibitions are only applied in situations where nothing else helps and under strict conditions.

117793.explanation: Borough officers must primarily be de-escalating and approachable. Arming them increases the chance of escalation and does not fit our vision of safety.

117795.explanation: To promote the flow of one- and two-person households out of single-family homes, this group is given priority when allocating social rental and/or new mid-priced apartments.

117798.explanation: We use existing space smartly by converting vacant buildings into homes, splitting existing homes, and building small-scale additions in neighborhoods. This ensures young people can stay in their community and the elderly can move more easily.

117799.explanation: New housing forms will be given space: cooperative living arrangements, flexible homes, and temporary housing. For urgent seekers and temporary tenants, we provide certainty and flow. Preventing homelessness is paramount.

117800.explanation: Our program states that trust and social security are central. People are on welfare not out of unwillingness but due to circumstances. We help people toward work with guidance, training, and tailored solutions. Mandatory counterperformance increases insecurity and often backfires.

117801.explanation: Tax waivers prevent people with low incomes from falling further into trouble. Stopping waivers increases debts and insecurity. We believe the municipality should protect financially vulnerable residents.

117806.explanation: No generic measure; however, we will adjust our parking rates for visitors to centers. This encourages seeking other transport methods. The funds raised will be invested in better accessibility with alternatives.

117807.explanation: Clean air is essential for health. With an environmental zone, we reduce emissions and protect residents against air pollution, with attention to feasible transitional arrangements.

117811.explanation: We choose a fair tax policy. Property tax (ozb) will be gradually increased towards the national average so that essential facilities and care remain affordable.

117814.explanation: We choose quality over quantity. Densification and sustainability of existing business parks are preferred over expansion at the expense of green spaces and living environment.

117816.explanation: Status holders are one of several groups given priority. This is necessary to enable integration and relieve reception locations, within fair and transparent rules.

117819.explanation: Sports and physical activity are important, and good sports facilities are necessary. Whether and how a swimming pool is renewed requires careful consideration of costs, usage, and alternatives.

117825.explanation: Village centers are places for meeting, support, and cohesion. They strengthen the livability in all communities.

117827.explanation: A low-traffic city center can have advantages, but only if accessibility and alternatives are well arranged. This requires tailored solutions and support.

117828.explanation: We want more affordable housing for starters and middle incomes. Affordable home ownership should also be part of a balanced housing mix in Bleiswijk.

117829.explanation: Youth work prevents problems and offers opportunities. In Bergschenhoek, sufficient deployment of youth workers is needed to support young people and prevent nuisance.

117769.explanation: A referendum, whether as an 'emergency brake' or to highlight a particular proposal, can work very well. D66 also supports the proposal of 'preferenda', where votes can be cast on different options.

117772.explanation: Shop owners should be able to choose as much as possible when they are open, to match the needs of their customers.

117773.explanation: D66 wants to support sustainable energy projects, such as wind, solar, and geothermal energy. We take into account the interests of local residents and future generations. We do this together with, among others, local energy cooperatives or through, for example, the sustainability loan.

117775.explanation: Lansingerland has a legal task to provide asylum reception under the Distribution Act. In addition, the temporary reception has so far run smoothly.

117778.explanation: Wood burning produces a lot of fine particulate matter, but a ban is unenforceable and therefore unrealistic.

117780.explanation: Fireworks cause a lot of nuisance and pollution of nature. The municipality should not actively support this. Alternative events such as a light and drone show are possible.

117781.explanation: Waste costs money to process and collect. If this is made free, everyone pays instead of the user. According to D66, that is unfair. You can bring your waste yourself without extra costs to the recycling station (environmental yard).

117782.explanation: Festivals in Lansingerland are very successful and rightly do not last deep into the night.

117783.explanation: The municipality may address entrepreneurs on this, but it is the entrepreneurs' responsibility to provide for it.

117787.explanation: D66 believes that subsidies for culture for low-income residents should remain available through the Youth Fund for Sports and Culture.

117791.explanation: D66 limits the use of cameras to temporary deployment at special moments, with clear justification beforehand and good evaluation afterward.

117793.explanation: Boa officers are for enforcing municipal rules, not for incidents involving potential violence. The police are responsible for that.

117795.explanation: Just as we all work, play sports, and recreate locally or regionally, Lansingerland builds both for the region and for its own residents. Currently, local residents already partly get priority, which we believe does not need to be further expanded.

117798.explanation: Vacant buildings while many people seek housing is unacceptable. However, there are very few office buildings in Lansingerland.

117799.explanation: Everyone yearns for suitable housing. Especially young people often do not get a chance: there are hardly any affordable homes, both in the (social) rental and owner-occupied sectors. Our housing supply does not meet the demand, and it is high time to build more suitable and affordable homes for all target groups.

117800.explanation: Encouraging a reciprocal contribution, such as volunteer work, is good for people receiving social assistance benefits but should never be mandatory.

117801.explanation: Solidarity with residents with a low income is important, especially in a wealthy municipality like Lansingerland.

117806.explanation: D66 supports forms of parking regulation, such as blue zones. In many existing neighborhoods there is sufficient parking space, so permits are not necessary. For new neighborhoods, this can be investigated, provided that there is enough public transport nearby as an alternative.

117807.explanation: In Lansingerland, village centers mainly see destination traffic. A low-emission zone therefore has little effect.

117811.explanation: D66 wants careful scrutiny of own expenditures first, before considering income increases. If very important facilities have to be cut, an increase in property tax is negotiable. However, this should not be automatic and must be well substantiated.

117814.explanation: Existing industrial areas may be renewed (under strict conditions), but there is no room in Lansingerland for designating new land for industrial sites.

117816.explanation: The asylum centers in the Netherlands are full, mainly because people with residence permits cannot find housing. Priority for housing is necessary to relieve pressure on asylum centers. Only a small portion of homes in the Netherlands is allocated to status holders (around 10% to 15%).

117819.explanation: The Windas is a modern swimming pool.

117825.explanation: Every village center deserves a place for culture and a library.

117827.explanation: The center of Bleiswijk would become safer and more lively with less car traffic. However, a good alternative route must be present.

117828.explanation: Everyone yearns for a suitable home. Especially young people often do not get a chance: there are hardly any affordable homes, both in the (social) rental and owner-occupied sectors. But many elderly also seek suitable, affordable housing. It is high time for more suitable and affordable homes!

117829.explanation: Our youth policy applies to all communities, and spending more money is not an end in itself.

117769.explanation: We have a representative democracy. In our view, representatives can make good decisions on important matters on behalf of the citizens.

117772.explanation: It is good to regulate this. Competing with ever later opening hours ultimately leads to increased nuisance on the streets during night hours.

117773.explanation: Much has already been stimulated in sustainable energy, for example through the net metering scheme. We are positive about supporting the energy transition, but not every initiative needs to be financially supported by the municipality.

117775.explanation: The Christian Union takes responsibility for the reception of newcomers.

117778.explanation: One of the biggest sources of poor air quality in the municipality is wood burning. In our view, we should increasingly restrict this. At the same time, we know many measures intervene directly in the personal life sphere of residents, and we should be cautious about that.

117780.explanation: Enforcing the national fireworks ban will be a major challenge. If an own fireworks show helps with this, we are willing to cooperate, although the environmental impact of fireworks is enormous.

117781.explanation: Collection is a good idea, but it does not have to be free. Free collection by the thrift store is cheaper and more environmentally friendly.

117782.explanation: A few years ago, we distributed 'hearing protection' at a festival in Lansingerland. Hearing damage from excessively loud music is common. Less loud music at festivals will undoubtedly have a positive effect on public health.

117783.explanation: Everyone participates. If the municipality must support making buildings accessible, then it will do so.

117787.explanation: Especially children from low-income families should be able to receive music lessons with subsidies. People who can afford it may pay for it themselves, as far as we are concerned.

117791.explanation: We are critical of camera surveillance. If its use is truly necessary for safety reasons, then it may be deployed, but it is an undesirable invasion of privacy and usually provides only a false sense of security. Therefore, we are cautious about it.

117793.explanation: This is not decided in the town hall of Lansingerland. Once the national government decides that enforcement officers should be armed, then we believe that is acceptable.

117795.explanation: We think this local connection is good. That is why we allocate as much as possible within the Woonnet Rijnmond scheme to our own residents.

117798.explanation: This can sometimes be a solution. Our municipality does not have much of this type of vacancy, so it barely plays a role.

117799.explanation: This can sometimes be a solution. Our municipality does not have many students, so it barely plays a role.

117800.explanation: It is good if people provide a counter-performance for their benefits; this can also offer a sense of purpose. However, the key lies in the word 'must'. Some people cannot provide a counter-performance, and then it becomes very uncomfortable. Encouraging is good, obliging is not.

117801.explanation: The municipality should not just forgive debt, but it must primarily provide assistance to people who are financially struggling.

117806.explanation: Paid parking is still far away in our municipality. We do not see it happening in the short term. As far as we are concerned, we have exhausted the possibilities for budget cuts. Then it is good to explore alternative sources of income, such as paid parking, starting at the metro stations.

117807.explanation: Our centers do not have the scale for a low-emission zone.

117811.explanation: We prefer to raise property tax (OZB) rather than pursue dismantling policies in the municipality. That said, there must be a concrete reason to increase the OZB; as long as there isn't one, it does not need to be raised, in our view.

117814.explanation: We have little space left in Lansingerland. We must handle the remaining space carefully. Additional space for businesses is not a priority for us.

117816.explanation: We have an obligation (task assignment) to house status holders. In our case, this is not possible without adequate priority.

117819.explanation: If there is reason to do so, then it must be done. Renewing it without cause is not necessary, in our opinion.

117825.explanation: We believe this is not currently an issue, and all communities are sufficiently provided with their own center for gatherings. Schools and churches can also play a good role here.

117827.explanation: The center of Bleiswijk is narrow. If making it car-free solves a concrete problem, then we are willing to consider it. However, at this moment we do not believe the problem is current.

117828.explanation: We are already doing this through construction along the Lange Vaart and Merenweg.

117829.explanation: As far as we are concerned, there is no concrete problem of too few youth workers in Bergschenhoek. Attention to youth work is important; therefore, we are not against it, but at this time no extra funding is necessary.

117769.explanation: Volt believes that important decisions must be made by elected representatives. They are elected to carefully weigh different interests and take responsibility for their choices.

117772.explanation: Volt is not in favor of unnecessary restrictions and locally we should not be needlessly restrictive. This way we ensure a lively village and a good balance with livability.

117773.explanation: If residents want to jointly generate sustainable energy, the municipality should financially support this. Local energy projects increase involvement and support within the neighborhood.

117775.explanation: Asylum reception in our municipality must be humane and well organized. Volt wants a fair distribution and spreading of refugees in the Netherlands, and an asylum policy that guarantees rights and guidance for people in need of protection, including social and legal assistance.

117778.explanation: Volt wants to work towards a healthy and sustainable living environment, with clean air and less emissions. Therefore, we believe use of wood stoves and open fireplaces should be limited.

117780.explanation: A municipal fireworks show provides a beautiful shared experience, instead of random blasts in every street. Volt has spoken out nationally for less consumer fireworks and more attention to safety and the environment.

117781.explanation: By not automatically collecting bulky waste for free at home, we encourage residents to better separate waste, reuse, and throw away less. Volt wants Lansingerland to support local initiatives that reduce waste, repair items, and recycle materials.

117782.explanation: Festivals and parties are important for culture, social interaction, and connection in Lansingerland. They create liveliness and bring people together. At the same time, we must consider neighbors. Instead of generally stricter noise standards, Volt prefers tailored solutions per event and location.

117783.explanation: Accessibility is important for an inclusive society. Through local subsidies, the municipality can motivate entrepreneurs to make their buildings accessible for people with disabilities. This helps the municipality showcase good examples and stimulates inclusive building and renovation.

117787.explanation: Cultural education gives children wings. Talent should not depend on parents' wallets. The municipality can ensure every primary school child can get music lessons through subsidies. Volt is well informed about European subsidies that can further support such initiatives.

117791.explanation: More camera surveillance is not always the best solution. It can come at the expense of privacy and trust in residents. Volt prefers other solutions, such as prevention, good lighting, a strong social environment, and visible presence of people in the neighborhood.

117793.explanation: Safety begins with trust and good contact with residents. Further arming of enforcement officers does not help with that. Volt prefers more visible presence, good training, and de-escalation. This way, officers can calmly resolve situations and Lansingerland remains a safe and open municipality.

117795.explanation: Living is not a postcode privilege. Social rental homes must be allocated fairly and transparently, based on need and waiting time, not on place of residence. Volt stands for equal opportunities for everyone and a fair housing market.

117798.explanation: We support repurposing vacant offices into housing, but not through automatic subsidies. Vacancy should be actively discouraged, for example, with a rising vacancy tax. The municipality can stimulate repurposing by speeding up permits and setting clear rules.

117799.explanation: Volt supports temporary modular student housing as a quick solution. However, we demand quality for reuse and good integration with the environment. Practical, sustainable, and livable: this is how Volt works on affordable housing in Lansingerland.

117800.explanation: Volt chooses trust and tailored solutions, not coercion. Motivation works better. We are positive about a basic income: security without a counter-performance. For equal opportunities and dignity in Lansingerland.

117801.explanation: People with low income deserve financial breathing room. Stopping debt forgiveness increases the chance of debts and stress. Volt chooses security and prevention. By keeping debt forgiveness, we prevent small worries from becoming big problems.

117806.explanation: With high parking pressure, permits ensure a livable neighborhood. Volt prioritizes safety over cars in residential streets. This way we create calm and space. Livability comes before unlimited accessibility in Lansingerland.

117807.explanation: We want cleaner air and a healthy living environment. At the same time, it is questionable whether a low-emission zone is currently necessary or appropriate for Lansingerland. We choose measures that work in our municipality and fit the local situation. Volt therefore looks soberly and realistically at what is needed.

117811.explanation: A limited increase in property tax may be necessary to maintain important facilities. The property tax in Lansingerland is below the national average. Volt sees taxes as a stable source of income, with sufficient support for a small increase to fund good facilities.

117814.explanation: We support expansion of business parks, but only under clear conditions. First, existing parks should be greened and renewed. Expansion must not come at the expense of nature and livability. Volt chooses sustainable, future-proof business activity with space for greenery.

117816.explanation: Volt supports priority access to social housing for recognized refugees to enable faster integration. This relieves asylum reception centers and ensures necessary flow-through. We choose a humane, fair, and well-organized approach to housing in Lansingerland.

117819.explanation: Sports are essential for health and social interaction. Volt wants to invest in safe, future-proof sports complexes such as De Windas. This strengthens the social foundation and keeps Lansingerland vital.

117825.explanation: Village centers are crucial for social interaction and safety. Volt Lansingerland chooses strong cores with local facilities where young, old, and associations come together. This strengthens social cohesion and livability in our villages.

117827.explanation: Car-restricted centers are safer and greener. Volt opts for space for pedestrians and cyclists, provided there are good alternatives such as robust public transport and accessibility for those who need it. Livability and safety are paramount in Lansingerland.

117828.explanation: Housing must be affordable and balanced. Volt does not choose one type of construction in Bleiswijk but advocates a mix of social rental, mid-rent, and owner-occupied housing in every area. This way, we build for all incomes and life stages throughout Lansingerland.

117829.explanation: Youth workers are crucial for opportunities and preventing nuisance. Volt wants this guidance not only in Bergschenhoek but in all villages of Lansingerland: so also Berkel and Bleiswijk. A municipality-wide approach for every young person.

statements.title: More affordable owner-occupied homes must be built in Bleiswijk.
The residents of Lansingerland must be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Lansingerland moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: Cafés and restaurants must be able to determine their own opening hours.
The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
The asylum reception center in our municipality should be closed as soon as possible.

titleParts.glossary: A place where people who have applied for asylum are accommodated. An asylum shelter can be temporary. A permanent asylum shelter is called an asylum seekers' centre (azc).

titleParts.text: in onze gemeente moet zo snel mogelijk sluiten.

statements.title: Noise standards at festivals and parties must be made stricter.
Lansingerland must introduce a parking permit in more neighborhoods.

titleParts.text: Lansingerland moet in meer wijken een

titleParts.glossary: A permit to park in a certain place. You have to pay for that right.

statements.title: In Lansingerland the use of open fireplaces and wood stoves must remain permitted.

titleParts.text: In Lansingerland moet het gebruik van open haarden en

titleParts.glossary: Wood-burning stoves used to heat a home.

statements.title: Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: All villages in the municipality must have their own village center.
The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

titleParts.text: helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: More camera surveillance must be introduced in Lansingerland.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: Vacant offices should be converted into housing with subsidies from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente worden omgebouwd tot woningen.

statements.title: The municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who oversee safety in Lansingerland must be equipped with a baton.

titleParts.glossary: Special Enforcement Officers. They can issue fines and often supervise the streets.

titleParts.text: die in Lansingerland controleren op veiligheid moeten een wapenstok krijgen.

statements.title: Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.
When allocating social housing, applicants from the municipality should be given priority.

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: moeten woningzoekenden uit de eigen gemeente voorrang krijgen.

statements.title: Lansingerland must introduce an environmental zone to exclude polluting vehicles from the center.

titleParts.glossary: An area where very polluting cars are not allowed to drive.

titleParts.text: invoeren om vervuilende voertuigen uit het centrum te weren.

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The property tax (ozb) should be increased.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: The municipality should give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: Business parks in Lansingerland may be expanded.
The center of Bleiswijk must become car-free.
The municipality must invest in temporary housing for students, for example container housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld steken in tijdelijke huisvesting voor studenten, bijvoorbeeld

titleParts.glossary: A movable and temporary dwelling, usually made from a shipping container.

statements.title: Lansingerland must renovate the De Windas swimming pool in Bergschenhoek.
The municipality must spend more money on youth workers in Bergschenhoek.
